title: pilosella densiflora subsp. ochrocephala
intro: Pilosella densiflora subsp. ochrocephala, also known as a type of yellow sedge or yellow mouse-ear, is a flowering plant species in the sunflower family, native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and as a diuretic, due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Pilosella densiflora subsp. ochrocephala include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used in traditional folk medicine to treat various health issues.
